{"action":"opened","issue":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/juliaup/issues/645","repository_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/juliaup","labels_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/juliaup/issues/645/labels{/name}","comments_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/juliaup/issues/645/comments","events_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/juliaup/issues/645/events","html_url":"https://github.com/JuliaLang/juliaup/issues/645","id":1716455024,"node_id":"I_kwDOEE6KR85mTwZw","number":645,"title":"`anyhow::bail!("Maybe we should never reach this?")`: what if we did reach this?","user":{"login":"mortenpi","id":147757,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjE0Nzc1Nw==","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/147757?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/mortenpi","html_url":"https://github.com/mortenpi","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mortenpi/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mortenpi/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mortenpi/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mortenpi/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mortenpi/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mortenpi/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mortenpi/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mortenpi/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mortenpi/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"labels":[],"state":"open","locked":false,"assignee":null,"assignees":[],"milestone":null,"comments":0,"created_at":"2023-05-19T00:56:39Z","updated_at":"2023-05-19T00:56:39Z","closed_at":null,"author_association":"NONE","active_lock_reason":null,"body":"I have an MWE that reaches this line:

https://github.com/JuliaLang/juliaup/blob/33d1fdbcc5369ac6e88d7d2b7254af38b271cdd0/src/bin/julialauncher.rs#L327

It involves interrupting an IO hang due to `--warn-overwrite=yes`. This came up in https://github.com/JuliaDocs/Documenter.jl/issues/2121. To reproduce, run the following script with IOCapture 0.2.2 (0.2.3 contains https://github.com/JuliaDocs/IOCapture.jl/pull/15 which removes the hang):

```julia
# julia --warn-overwrite=yes --project script.jl
using IOCapture
println("The following will hang forever... Ctrl+C now.")
c = IOCapture.capture() do
    for i in 1:1024
        eval(:(function f() 1 end))
    end
end
```

This must be run as a script, i.e. `julia --warn-overwrite=yes --project script.jl` (where `julia` is `juliaup`). I have the correct manifest here: https://gist.github.com/mortenpi/6223ef6f3c02a1b9fbfe2530a0d884e3

It should result in

```
$ julia --warn-overwrite=yes script.jl --project script.jl
The following will hang forever... Ctrl+C now.
^CError: Maybe we should never reach this?
```

This is on Linux. And it seems like the Julia version matters -- I get this with the official 1.9 binary, but not with 1.7 or 1.8. I didn't try to master.

```
julia> versioninfo()
Julia Version 1.9.0
Commit 8e630552924 (2023-05-07 11:25 UTC)
Platform Info:
  OS: Linux (x86_64-linux-gnu)
  CPU: 16 × 12th Gen Intel(R) Core(TM) i7-1260P
  WORD_SIZE: 64
  LIBM: libopenlibm
  LLVM: libLLVM-14.0.6 (ORCJIT, alderlake)
  Threads: 1 on 16 virtual cores
```

It isn't obvious to me how to make a new `` ```@foo `` block without editing the Documenter source code.

Eee... so about that :sweat_smile: The short answer is sort of, but not officially? I want proper plugin support, but right now you just have to add the appropriate method overloads, basically. In general, I want to encourage more experimentation with plugins, and then once we have some use cases, we can formulate proper, official APIs in Documenter for them (that adhere to semver and have strict interfaces and all that).

It is doable as a separate package though. Here's a simple package that matches the `mermaid` code blocks and treats them appropriately (including an overload of `Documenter.HTMLWriter.domify` to actually render HTML): https://github.com/JuliaDocs/DocumenterMermaid.jl/blob/main/src/DocumenterMermaid.jl

The API reference, I imagine, would hook into the build process somehow differently though. {"action":"created","comment":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/jump-dev/JuMP.jl/pulls/comments/1211333209","pull_request_review_id":1452576827,"id":1211333209,"node_id":"PRRC_kwDOAF3Xtc5IM3pZ","diff_hunk":"@@ -0,0 +1,182 @@
+#  Copyright 2023, Oscar Dowson and contributors
+#  This Source Code Form is subject to the terms of the Mozilla Public
+#  License, v. 2.0. If a copy of the MPL was not distributed with this
+#  file, You can obtain one at https://mozilla.org/MPL/2.0/.
+
+module DocumenterReference
+
+import Documenter
+import Markdown
+import MarkdownAST
+
+@enum(
+    DocType,
+    DOCTYPE_ABSTRACT_TYPE,
+    DOCTYPE_CONSTANT,
+    DOCTYPE_FUNCTION,
+    DOCTYPE_MACRO,
+    DOCTYPE_STRUCT,
+)
+
+struct _Config
+    current_module::Module
+    root::String
+    subdirectory::String
+    extras::Vector{Pair{String,DocType}}
+end
+
+const CONFIG = _Config[]
+
+abstract type APIBuilder <: Documenter.Builder.DocumentPipeline end
+
+Documenter.Selectors.order(::Type{APIBuilder}) = 0.0
+
+"""
+    automatic_reference_documentation(
+        current_module::Module;
+        root::String,
+        subdirectory::String,
+        extras::Vector{Pair{String,DocType}} = Pair{String,DocType}[],
+    )
+
+Automatically creates the API reference documentation for `current_module` and
+returns a `Vector` which can be used in the `pages` argument of
+`Documenter.makedocs`.
+
+## Arguments
+
+ * `current_module`: the module from which to create an API reference.
+ * `subdirectory`: the directory relative to the documentation root in which to
+   write the API files.
+ * `extras`: a vector of non-exported docstrings to include in the API
+   reference. Each element is a pair which maps the docstring signature to a
+   [`DocumenterReference.DocType`](@ref) enum.
+
+## Multiple instances
+
+Each time you call this function, a new object is added to the global variable
+`DocumenterReference.CONFIG`.
+"""
+function automatic_reference_documentation(
+    current_module::Module;
+    root::String,
+    subdirectory::String,
+    extras::Vector{Pair{String,DocType}} = Pair{String,DocType}[],
+)
+    config = _Config(current_module, root, subdirectory, extras)
+    pages = Any["Overview"=>"$subdirectory/$current_module.md"]
+    _iterate_over_symbols(config) do key, type
+        push!(pages, Documenter.hide("`$key`" => "$subdirectory/$key.md"))
+        return
+    end
+    push!(CONFIG, config)
+    return pages
+end
+
+function _exported_symbols(mod)
+    contents = Pair{Symbol,DocType}[]
+    for n in names(mod)
+        f = getfield(mod, n)
+        f_str = string(f)
+        if startswith(f_str, "@")
+            push!(contents, n => DOCTYPE_MACRO)
+        elseif startswith(f_str, "Abstract")
+            push!(contents, n => DOCTYPE_ABSTRACT_TYPE)
+        elseif f isa Type
+            push!(contents, n => DOCTYPE_STRUCT)
+        elseif f isa Function
+            if islowercase(f_str[1])
+                push!(contents, n => DOCTYPE_FUNCTION)
+            else
+                push!(contents, n => DOCTYPE_STRUCT)
+            end
+        elseif f isa Module
+            # Skip
+        else
+            push!(contents, n => DOCTYPE_CONSTANT)
+        end
+    end
+    order = Dict(
+        DOCTYPE_MACRO => 1,
+        DOCTYPE_FUNCTION => 2,
+        DOCTYPE_ABSTRACT_TYPE => 3,
+        DOCTYPE_STRUCT => 4,
+        DOCTYPE_CONSTANT => 5,
+    )
+    return sort(contents; by = x -> (order[x[2]], "$(x[1])"))
+end
+
+function _iterate_over_symbols(f, config)
+    current_module = config.current_module
+    for (key, type) in vcat(_exported_symbols(current_module), config.extras)
+        if key isa Symbol
+            doc = Base.Docs.doc(Base.Docs.Binding(current_module, key))
+            if occursin("No documentation found.", string(doc))
+                if type == DOCTYPE_CONSTANT
+                    continue
+                else
+                    error("Documentation missing for $key")
+                end
+            end
+        end
+        f(key, type)
+    end
+    return
+end
+
+function _to_string(x::DocType)
+    if x == DOCTYPE_ABSTRACT_TYPE
+        return "abstract type"
+    elseif x == DOCTYPE_CONSTANT
+        return "constant"
+    elseif x == DOCTYPE_FUNCTION
+        return "function"
+    elseif x == DOCTYPE_MACRO
+        return "macro"
+    elseif x == DOCTYPE_STRUCT
+        return "struct"
+    end
+end
+
+function _add_page(document::Documenter.Document, filename, contents)
+    mdpage = Markdown.parse(contents)
+    document.blueprint.pages[filename] = Documenter.Page(
+        filename, # source,
+        "$(document.user.build)/$filename", # build,
+        "$(document.user.build)/", # workdir,
+        mdpage.content,
+        Documenter.Globals(),
+        convert(MarkdownAST.Node, mdpage),
+    )
+    return
+end
+

+    subdir = config.subdirectory
+    overview_md = """

+#  Copyright 2023, Oscar Dowson and contributors
+#  This Source Code Form is subject to the terms of the Mozilla Public
+#  License, v. 2.0. If a copy of the MPL was not distributed with this
+#  file, You can obtain one at https://mozilla.org/MPL/2.0/.
+
+module DocumenterReference
+
+import Documenter
+import Markdown
+import MarkdownAST
+
+@enum(
+    DocType,
+    DOCTYPE_ABSTRACT_TYPE,
+    DOCTYPE_CONSTANT,
+    DOCTYPE_FUNCTION,
+    DOCTYPE_MACRO,
+    DOCTYPE_STRUCT,
+)
+
+struct _Config
+    current_module::Module
+    root::String
+    subdirectory::String
+    extras::Vector{Pair{String,DocType}}
+end
+
+const CONFIG = _Config[]
+
+abstract type APIBuilder <: Documenter.Builder.DocumentPipeline end
+
+Documenter.Selectors.order(::Type{APIBuilder}) = 0.0
+
+"""
+    automatic_reference_documentation(
+        current_module::Module;
+        root::String,
+        subdirectory::String,
+        extras::Vector{Pair{String,DocType}} = Pair{String,DocType}[],
+    )
+
+Automatically creates the API reference documentation for `current_module` and
+returns a `Vector` which can be used in the `pages` argument of
+`Documenter.makedocs`.
+
+## Arguments
+
+ * `current_module`: the module from which to create an API reference.
+ * `subdirectory`: the directory relative to the documentation root in which to
+   write the API files.
+ * `extras`: a vector of non-exported docstrings to include in the API
+   reference. Each element is a pair which maps the docstring signature to a
+   [`DocumenterReference.DocType`](@ref) enum.
+
+## Multiple instances
+
+Each time you call this function, a new object is added to the global variable
+`DocumenterReference.CONFIG`.
+"""
+function automatic_reference_documentation(
+    current_module::Module;
+    root::String,
+    subdirectory::String,
+    extras::Vector{Pair{String,DocType}} = Pair{String,DocType}[],
+)
+    config = _Config(current_module, root, subdirectory, extras)
+    pages = Any["Overview"=>"$subdirectory/$current_module.md"]
+    _iterate_over_symbols(config) do key, type
+        push!(pages, Documenter.hide("`$key`" => "$subdirectory/$key.md"))
+        return
+    end
+    push!(CONFIG, config)
+    return pages
+end
+
+function _exported_symbols(mod)
+    contents = Pair{Symbol,DocType}[]
+    for n in names(mod)
+        f = getfield(mod, n)
+        f_str = string(f)
+        if startswith(f_str, "@")
+            push!(contents, n => DOCTYPE_MACRO)
+        elseif startswith(f_str, "Abstract")
+            push!(contents, n => DOCTYPE_ABSTRACT_TYPE)
+        elseif f isa Type
+            push!(contents, n => DOCTYPE_STRUCT)
+        elseif f isa Function
+            if islowercase(f_str[1])
+                push!(contents, n => DOCTYPE_FUNCTION)
+            else
+                push!(contents, n => DOCTYPE_STRUCT)
+            end
+        elseif f isa Module
+            # Skip
+        else
+            push!(contents, n => DOCTYPE_CONSTANT)
+        end
+    end
+    order = Dict(
+        DOCTYPE_MACRO => 1,
+        DOCTYPE_FUNCTION => 2,
+        DOCTYPE_ABSTRACT_TYPE => 3,
+        DOCTYPE_STRUCT => 4,
+        DOCTYPE_CONSTANT => 5,
+    )
+    return sort(contents; by = x -> (order[x[2]], "$(x[1])"))
+end
+
+function _iterate_over_symbols(f, config)
+    current_module = config.current_module
+    for (key, type) in vcat(_exported_symbols(current_module), config.extras)
+        if key isa Symbol
+            doc = Base.Docs.doc(Base.Docs.Binding(current_module, key))
+            if occursin("No documentation found.", string(doc))
+                if type == DOCTYPE_CONSTANT
+                    continue
+                else
+                    error("Documentation missing for $key")
+                end
+            end
+        end
+        f(key, type)
+    end
+    return
+end
+
+function _to_string(x::DocType)
+    if x == DOCTYPE_ABSTRACT_TYPE
+        return "abstract type"
+    elseif x == DOCTYPE_CONSTANT
+        return "constant"
+    elseif x == DOCTYPE_FUNCTION
+        return "function"
+    elseif x == DOCTYPE_MACRO
+        return "macro"
+    elseif x == DOCTYPE_STRUCT
+        return "struct"
+    end
+end
+
+function _add_page(document::Documenter.Document, filename, contents)
+    mdpage = Markdown.parse(contents)
+    document.blueprint.pages[filename] = Documenter.Page(
+        filename, # source,
+        "$(document.user.build)/$filename", # build,
+        "$(document.user.build)/", # workdir,
+        mdpage.content,
+        Documenter.Globals(),

```julia

@precompile_all_calls begin
    IOCapture.capture() do
        println("...")
    end
end
```

Leads to a segfault with 1.10-DEV after e06a5915a9e93ed5d4c25cf819275af18adf8187 (#44527 and #47407, cc @timholy @vchuravy; 328dd578958d9c2a22ddb11970324ecd04e94314 is still good; thanks to @inkydragon for bisecting!).

It can be reproduced by running a simple snippet in the _test environment_ (I can't replicate it in the normal environment..).

```julia
IOCapture.capture() do
    println("println")
end
```

I have [pushed a branch with an MWE](https://github.com/JuliaDocs/IOCapture.jl/compare/mp/precompile-mwe) and doing `julia --project -e'using Pkg; Pkg.test()'` with Julia master should reproduce it.

While the error is different, I think it's related to our precompile woes in Documenter where a SnoopPrecompile leads to a strange error after e06a5915a9e93ed5d4c25cf819275af18adf8187 that also traces It can be reproduced by running a simple snippet in the _test environment_ (I can't replicate it in the normal environment..).

```julia
IOCapture.capture() do
    println("println")
end
```

I have [pushed a branch with an MWE](https://github.com/JuliaDocs/IOCapture.jl/compare/mp/precompile-mwe) and doing `julia --project -e'using Pkg; Pkg.test()'` with Julia master should reproduce it.

While the error is different, I think it's related to our precompile woes in Documenter where a SnoopPrecompile leads to a strange error after e06a5915a9e93ed5d4c25cf819275af18adf8187 that also traces back to IOCapture usage: https://github.com/JuliaDocs/Documenter.jl/pull/2004","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/48837/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"timeline_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/48837/timeline","performed_via_github_app":null,"state_reason":"reopened"}}

don't know if this is a SnoopPrecompile (v1.0.3) issue or a Julia one, but adding the following call to IOCapture.jl:

```julia
using SnoopPrecompile
@precompile_all_calls begin
    IOCapture.capture() do
        println("...")
    end
end
```

Leads to a segfault with 1.10-DEV after e06a5915a9e93ed5d4c25cf819275af18adf8187 (#44527 and #47407, cc @timholy @vchuravy; 328dd578958d9c2a22ddb11970324ecd04e94314 is still good; thanks to @inkydragon for bisecting!).

end
```

I have [pushed a branch with an MWE](https://github.com/JuliaDocs/IOCapture.jl/compare/mp/precompile-mwe) and doing `julia --project -e'using Pkg; Pkg.test()'` with Julia master should reproduce it.

While the error is different, I think it's related to our precompile woes in Documenter where a SnoopPrecompile leads to a strange error after e06a5915a9e93ed5d4c25cf819275af18adf8187 that also traces This unclosed code blocks are perfectly valid Markdown.

I suppose, in principle, we could detect paragraph that start with backticks, as that is _likely_ to be an error:

````
julia> using Markdown

julia> m = md"""
       XYZ

       ```julia
       xyz
       """
  XYZ

  ```julia xyz

julia> m.content
2-element Vector{Any}:
 Markdown.Paragraph(Any["XYZ"])
 Markdown.Paragraph(Any["```julia xyz"])
````

Actually, that might be reasonably safe, since if you escape the fences, you actually get a slightly different AST:

````
julia> m = md"""
       XYZ

       \```julia
       xyz

       ```
       """
  XYZ

  ```julia xyz

  ```

julia> m.content
3-element Vector{Any}:
 Markdown.Paragraph(Any["XYZ"])
 Markdown.Paragraph(Any["`", "``julia xyz"])
 Markdown.Paragraph(Any["```"])
````","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/comments/1455252573/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"performed_via_github_app":null}}

```julia
IOCapture.capture() do
    println("println")
end
```

I have [pushed a branch with an MWE](https://github.com/JuliaDocs/IOCapture.jl/compare/mp/precompile-mwe) and doing `julia --project -e'using Pkg; Pkg.test()'` with Julia master should reproduce it.

While the error is different, I think it's related to our precompile woes in Documenter where a SnoopPrecompile leads to a strange error after e06a5915a9e93ed5d4c25cf819275af18adf8187 that also traces back to IOCapture usage: https://github.com/JuliaDocs/Documenter.jl/pull/2004","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/48837/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"timeline_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/48837/timeline","performed_via_github_app":null,"state_reason":"completed"},"comment":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/comments/1455263971","html_url":"https://github.com/JuliaLang/julia/issues/48837#issuecomment-1455263971","issue_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/48837","id":1455263971,"node_id":"IC_kwDOABkWpM5WvZDj","user":{"login":"mortenpi","id":147757,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjE0Nzc1Nw==","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/147757?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/mortenpi","html_url":"https://github.com/mortenpi","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mortenpi/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mortenpi/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mortenpi/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mortenpi/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mortenpi/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mortenpi/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mortenpi/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mortenpi/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mortenpi/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"created_at":"2023-03-06T00:24:07Z","updated_at":"2023-03-06T00:24:07Z","author_association":"CONTRIBUTOR","body":"Ah, sorry, I didn't notice the other PR. ```
julia> Pkg.add(PackageSpec(name="Example", uuid="8dfed614-e22c-5e08-85e1-65c5234f0b41"))
ERROR: Refusing to add package `Example [8dfed614]`.
Package `Example=8dfed614-e22c-5e08-85e1-65c5234f0b40` with the same name already exists as a direct dependency.
To remove the existing package, use `import Pkg; Pkg.rm("Example")`.
```

But I now realize that this is not the case:

```
julia> Pkg.add(PackageSpec(name="Foobar", uuid="8dfed614-e22c-5e08-85e1-65c5234f0b40"))
   Resolving package versions...
  No Changes to `/tmp/jl_s7Kgmp/Project.toml`
  No Changes to `/tmp/jl_s7Kgmp/Manifest.toml`
  0 dependencies successfully precompiled in 0 seconds
  1 dependency errored. To see a full report either run `import Pkg; Pkg.precompile()` or load the package

(jl_s7Kgmp) pkg> st
Status `/tmp/jl_s7Kgmp/Project.toml`
→ [8dfed614] Foobar
Info Packages marked with → are not downloaded, use `instantiate` to download
```

Instead, the environment seems to end up in some inconsistent state.. So now I am no longer exactly sure what should happen here, but I can see two possible actionable items:

1. Pkg should verify the UUID if it is specified and it downloads a package from somewhere.
2. Consider this a request to improve the error message. It should explicitly say that you shouldn't specify `uuid` and `url` together (and I guess some other combinations as well).","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/Pkg.jl/issues/comments/1473518073/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"performed_via_github_app":null}}

{"action":"opened","issue":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/48731","repository_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia","labels_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/48731/labels{/name}","comments_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/48731/comments","events_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/48731/events","html_url":"https://github.com/JuliaLang/julia/issues/48731","id":1591062893,"node_id":"I_kwDOABkWpM5e1bFt","number":48731,"title":"Clearer error message when unable to determine exact type parameter value in a method","user":{"login":"mortenpi","id":147757,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjE0Nzc1Nw==","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/147757?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/mortenpi","html_url":"https://github.com/mortenpi","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mortenpi/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mortenpi/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mortenpi/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mortenpi/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mortenpi/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mortenpi/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mortenpi/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mortenpi/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mortenpi/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"labels":[],"state":"open","locked":false,"assignee":null,"assignees":[],"milestone":null,"comments":0,"created_at":"2023-02-20T03:24:11Z","updated_at":"2023-02-20T03:24:11Z","closed_at":null,"author_association":"CONTRIBUTOR","active_lock_reason":null,"body":"It is possible to define methods where Julia is unable to determine a concrete value for a type parameter, leading to a `UndefVarError`:

```julia
julia> foo(::Ref{<:T}) where T = T
foo (generic function with 1 method)

julia> foo(Ref{String}())
String

julia> foo(Ref{AbstractString}())
ERROR: UndefVarError: `T` not defined
```

The error is confusing since, from a user's perspective, `T` is very much defined/declared in the `where` part of the method definition.

Rather, could we throw a dedicated `AmbiguousTypeParameter` error here (bikeshed, please)? Preferably with a docstring that would explain that it's possible to construct signatures where you can't uniquely figure out a type. And would also probably suggest avoiding unnecessary subtyping in method signature (like in the `Ref{<:T}` example above), since that is most likely an error on the user's part.

A couple of related issues I was able to find: https://github.com/JuliaLang/julia/issues/25307, https://github.com/JuliaLang/julia/issues/39277, https://github.com/JuliaLang/julia/issues/39280, https://github.com/JuliaLang/julia/issues/41841, https://github.com/JuliaLang/julia/issues/41728. It also caused some discussion [on Zulip recently](https://julialang.zulipchat.com/#narrow/stream/225542-helpdesk/topic/.E2.9C.94.20Spooky.20error.20with.20parametric.20types.20in.20tuples).

```julia
using SnoopPrecompile
@precompile_all_calls begin
    IOCapture.capture() do
        println("...")
    end
end
```

Leads to a segfault with 1.10-DEV after e06a5915a9e93ed5d4c25cf819275af18adf8187 (#44527 and #47407, cc @timholy @vchuravy; 328dd578958d9c2a22ddb11970324ecd04e94314 is still good; thanks to @inkydragon for bisecting!).

It can be reproduced by running a simple snippet in the _test environment_ (I can't replicate it in the normal environment..).

```julia
IOCapture.capture() do
    println("println")
end
```

I have [pushed a branch with an MWE](https://github.com/JuliaDocs/IOCapture.jl/compare/mp/precompile-mwe) and doing `julia --project -e'using Pkg; Pkg.test()'` with Julia master should reproduce it.

While the error is different, I think it's related to our precompile woes in Documenter where a SnoopPrecompile leads to a strange error after e06a5915a9e93ed5d4c25cf819275af18adf8187 that also traces back to IOCapture usage: https://github.com/JuliaDocs/Documenter.jl/pull/2004","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/48837/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"timeline_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/48837/timeline","performed_via_github_app":null,"state_reason":null}}

I presume it will also break code loading if I `pkg> add`, `cd` and then do `using`.

For this reason, I would actually advocate turning any modifications to `DEPOT_PATH` into absolute paths, even when done from within Julia. If you really-really want your depot to change when you `cd` within Julia, you can always explicitly pop-push `DEPOT_PATH`.

```
~/test$ JULIA_DEPOT_PATH=depot julia

julia> mkdir("foo"); cd("foo")

(@v1.9) pkg> registry add
  Installing known registries into `depot`
  
julia> cd(".."); mkdir("bar"); cd("bar")

(@v1.9) pkg> add Example
  Installing known registries into `depot`
    Updating registry at `depot/registries/General`
    Updating git-repo `https://github.com/JuliaRegistries/General.git`
   Resolving package versions...
   Installed Example ─ v0.5.3
    Updating `~/test/bar/depot/environments/v1.9/Project.toml`

~/test$ tree -L 3
.
├── bar
│   └── depot
│       ├── compiled
│       ├── environments
│       ├── logs
│       ├── packages
│       ├── registries
│       └── scratchspaces
├── depot
│   └── logs
│       └── repl_history.jl
└── foo
    └── depot
        └── registries
```","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/comments/1301585486/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"performed_via_github_app":null}}

sorry, you are right. The renaming logic works by passing this

https://github.com/JuliaLang/docs.julialang.org/blob/16a515e71f2ea6bb2b18a68eb510ab453db35f32/pdf/make.jl#L74

which has the version number, to this

https://github.com/JuliaLang/docs.julialang.org/blob/16a515e71f2ea6bb2b18a68eb510ab453db35f32/pdf/make.jl#L58-L69

where we look for any PDF and rename it to `file`. The simplest solution might be to run a small Julia script on Buildkite that checks if it's a tagged Julia, like we do [in `doc/make.jl` to determine if it is a tagged version or not](https://github.com/JuliaLang/julia/blob/fa9c1370992bb2d9d017844934bda18f8a376586/doc/make.jl#L347-L354).

Although I guess a more robust solution would be to handle it in `doc/make.jl`? The Julia docs build will indeed always produce `TheJuliaLanguage.pdf` right now. We use [the `.version` field for the version](https://github.com/JuliaDocs/Documenter.jl/blob/998d26e568a20e3b1764d0c2cef30ebe4d2563ae/src/Writers/LaTeXWriter.jl#L169), which we [could determine from `TRAVIS_TAG`](https://github.com/JuliaDocs/Documenter.jl/blob/998d26e568a20e3b1764d0c2cef30ebe4d2563ae/src/Writers/LaTeXWriter.jl#L57) since [we do not explicitly set in the Julia `make.jl`](https://github.com/JuliaLang/julia/blob/fa9c1370992bb2d9d017844934bda18f8a376586/doc/make.jl#L277-L279), but `TRAVIS_TAG` is not set anywhere. So we will never attach the version number to the filename in Documenter. We could also set the `version` option to `LaTeX` there.","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/docs.julialang.org/issues/comments/1287963238/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"performed_via_github_app":null}}

bug"}],"state":"closed","locked":false,"assignee":null,"assignees":[],"milestone":null,"comments":3,"created_at":"2022-07-16T13:53:12Z","updated_at":"2022-10-31T08:53:40Z","closed_at":"2022-08-31T12:21:08Z","author_association":"MEMBER","active_lock_reason":null,"draft":false,"pull_request":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/pulls/46068","html_url":"https://github.com/JuliaLang/julia/pull/46068","diff_url":"https://github.com/JuliaLang/julia/pull/46068.diff","patch_url":"https://github.com/JuliaLang/julia/pull/46068.patch","merged_at":"2022-08-31T12:21:08Z"},"body":"This fixes the two cases given in https://github.com/JuliaLang/julia/issues/45977 where the wrong package version gets loaded.

The first case is where the package version is not found in the depot. In that case, we now return a `missing` to signal not to look any further through the env stack.

The second case is where a manifest entry is not present for a package identified in the current env. This is fixed by returning the env a package got identified with and then stopping searching through envs in `locate_package` when that env is hit.

 Even if you have never `add`ed `D` directly, this kind of error can arise
 if `D` is required by other packages that you are trying to use.
 
+!!! note
+    When tackling these conflicts, first consider that the bigger a project gets, the more likely this is to happen.
+    Using targeted projects for a given task is highly recommended, and removing unused dependencies is a good first
+    step when hitting these issues.
+    For instance, a common pitfall is having more than a few packages in your default (i.e. `(@1.7)`) environment,","path":"docs/src/managing-packages.md","position":null,"original_position":8,"commit_id":"9da6c48c8cc2bb0c5d2302f5d2e3f8204a680151","original_commit_id":"433cc9ca1905cea6d170c5e8d693bf130127287d","user":{"login":"mortenpi","id":147757,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjE0Nzc1Nw==","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/147757?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/mortenpi","html_url":"https://github.com/mortenpi","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mortenpi/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mortenpi/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mortenpi/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mortenpi/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mortenpi/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mortenpi/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mortenpi/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mortenpi/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mortenpi/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"body":"If this was a block (e.g. a whole paragraph) then you could use an at-eval block, but unfortunately we can't really interpolate into an inline context like this. {"action":"opened","issue":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/46251","repository_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia","labels_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/46251/labels{/name}","comments_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/46251/comments","events_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/46251/events","html_url":"https://github.com/JuliaLang/julia/issues/46251","id":1328433940,"node_id":"I_kwDOABkWpM5PLksU","number":46251,"title":"Assignment in tuples, arrays","user":{"login":"mortenpi","id":147757,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjE0Nzc1Nw==","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/147757?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/mortenpi","html_url":"https://github.com/mortenpi","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mortenpi/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mortenpi/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mortenpi/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mortenpi/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mortenpi/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mortenpi/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mortenpi/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mortenpi/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mortenpi/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"labels":[],"state":"open","locked":false,"assignee":null,"assignees":[],"milestone":null,"comments":0,"created_at":"2022-08-04T10:45:39Z","updated_at":"2022-08-04T10:45:39Z","closed_at":null,"author_association":"CONTRIBUTOR","active_lock_reason":null,"body":"Assignment in tuples and arrays

```
julia> x=65; [x,x|=0x20]
2-element Vector{Int64}:
 97
 97

julia> x=65; (x,x|=0x20)
(97, 97)
```

was made a syntax error at one point (in 0.3; see #4982, #4990 and closing commits), but it's allowed again (not sure if intentionally or not). But it leads to some fun inconsistencies:

```

in tuples and arrays

```
julia> x=65; [x,x|=0x20]
2-element Vector{Int64}:
 97
 97

julia> x=65; (x,x|=0x20)
(97, 97)
```

was made a syntax error at one point (in 0.3; see #4982, #4990 and closing commits), but it's allowed again (not sure if intentionally or not). But it leads to some fun inconsistencies:

```
julia> value = 1; (value, value + 1, value += 1, value += 1)
(3, 2, 2, 3)

julia> let value = 1; (value, value + 1, value += 1, value += 1); end
(1, 2, 2, 3)
```","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/46251/reactions","total_count":1,"+1":1,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"timeline_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/46251/timeline","performed_via_github_app":null,"state_reason":null},"comment":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/comments/1206160050","html_url":"https://github.com/JuliaLang/julia/issues/46251#issuecomment-1206160050","issue_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/46251","id":1206160050,"node_id":"IC_kwDOABkWpM5H5Iqy","user":{"login":"mortenpi","id":147757,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjE0Nzc1Nw==","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/147757?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/mortenpi","html_url":"https://github.com/mortenpi","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mortenpi/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mortenpi/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mortenpi/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mortenpi/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mortenpi/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mortenpi/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mortenpi/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mortenpi/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mortenpi/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"created_at":"2022-08-05T07:59:02Z","updated_at":"2022-08-05T07:59:02Z","author_association":"CONTRIBUTOR","body":"I guess it's perhaps not surprising, but I noticed that this also affects string interpolation:

QuickSort","user":{"login":"LilithHafner","id":60898866,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjYwODk4ODY2","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/60898866?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/LilithHafner","html_url":"https://github.com/LilithHafner","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/LilithHafner/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/LilithHafner/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/LilithHafner/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/LilithHafner/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/LilithHafner/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/LilithHafner/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/LilithHafner/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/LilithHafner/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/LilithHafner/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"body":""Quicksort is unstable and attempts to stabilize it hurt performance" (citation needed). I believe I have simultaneously stabilized and sped up quicksort, which should allow us to simplify sorting policy. Instability comes from the partition algorithm. We currently use something like this ```julia while i < j while(v[i] < pivot) i += 1 end while(v[j] > pivot) j -= 1 end v[i], v[j] = v[j], v[i] end ``` Which is an efficient in place unstable partition algorithm. If we allow ourselves scratch space to conduct the partition, we can use something like this instead ```julia for x in v if x < pivot t[i] = x i += 1 else t[j] = x j -= 1 end end ``` This new approach is still unstable, but it is predictably unstable. Everything before the pivot is stable, and everything after it is reversed. After several nested partitions, we still only need to reverse half the elements one time to reintroduce stability. [Benchmarks](https://gist.github.com/LilithHafner/56fa5b3e4ed984bcd5af086adbcb98b8) reveal that this approach is typically faster than the original, possibly because the order of comparisons and computations is known at the beginning of the partition rather than depending on the branches during the partition. In effect, the branches in this new algorithm have less impact on future code execution. I have not profiled this implementation at the CPU level and don't know for sure why it is a speedup. Caveat 1: I benchmarked on a somewhat inconsistently noisy machine. Caveat 2: This is sometimes a slowdown. See graphs. Future work indicated by this PR includes - Move MergeSort to SortingAlgorithms.jl - Now that every sorting algorithm in Base's sorting policy except InsertionSort uses scratch space, it becomes even more valuable to effectively pass scratch-space vectors around. For example, in this PR, fpsort! results in redundant scratch space allocations. - We partition in quicksort and fpsort!. Generalizing partition! and factoring it out should result in substantial readability improvements in fpsort! which, in turn, should facilitate extending support optimized sorting for unions with Missing to all vectors. We could also export `partition!`. Closes #11429 Closes optimize, and increase robustness of QuickSort","user":{"login":"LilithHafner","id":60898866,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjYwODk4ODY2","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/60898866?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/LilithHafner","html_url":"https://github.com/LilithHafner","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/LilithHafner/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/LilithHafner/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/LilithHafner/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/LilithHafner/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/LilithHafner/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/LilithHafner/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/LilithHafner/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/LilithHafner/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/LilithHafner/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"body":""Quicksort is unstable and attempts to stabilize it hurt performance" (citation needed). I believe I have simultaneously stabilized and sped up quicksort, which should allow us to simplify sorting policy. Instability comes from the partition algorithm. We currently use something like this ```julia while i < j while(v[i] < pivot) i += 1 end while(v[j] > pivot) j -= 1 end v[i], v[j] = v[j], v[i] end ``` Which is an efficient in place unstable partition algorithm. If we allow ourselves scratch space to conduct the partition, we can use something like this instead ```julia for x in v if x < pivot t[i] = x i += 1 else t[j] = x j -= 1 end end ``` This new approach is still unstable, but it is predictably unstable. Everything before the pivot is stable, and everything after it is reversed. After several nested partitions, we still only need to reverse half the elements one time to reintroduce stability. [Benchmarks](https://gist.github.com/LilithHafner/56fa5b3e4ed984bcd5af086adbcb98b8) reveal that this approach is typically faster than the original, possibly because the order of comparisons and computations is known at the beginning of the partition rather than depending on the branches during the partition. In effect, the branches in this new algorithm have less impact on future code execution. I have not profiled this implementation at the CPU level and don't know for sure why it is a speedup. Caveat 1: I benchmarked on a somewhat inconsistently noisy machine. Caveat 2: This is sometimes a slowdown. See graphs. Future work indicated by this PR includes - Move MergeSort to SortingAlgorithms.jl - Now that every sorting algorithm in Base's sorting policy except InsertionSort uses scratch space, it becomes even more valuable to effectively pass scratch-space vectors around. For example, in this PR, fpsort! results in redundant scratch space allocations. - We partition in quicksort and fpsort!. Generalizing partition! and factoring it out should result in substantial readability improvements in fpsort! which, in turn, should facilitate extending support optimized sorting for unions with Missing to all vectors. We could also export `partition!`. Closes #11429 Closes is the simpler part of PR #45367. In essence the small change in `Base.Docs.astname` solves (as far as I see) issue #44889. Furthermore the change in `REPL.lookup_doc` allows to access documentation of an expression which contains a `where`. A minimal all in one example would be

```julia
julia> struct MyParametricFunc{T} end
julia> "Some docs"
julia> (f::MyParametricFunc{T})() where T = f
julia> @doc (::MyParametricFunc{T})() where T
Some docs
```

This only fixes the name of the documented function, not the signature, but at least it allows signatures including type parameters and is the simpler part of PR #45367. In essence the small change in `Base.Docs.astname` solves (as far as I see) issue #44889. Furthermore the change in `REPL.lookup_doc` allows to access documentation of an expression which contains a `where`. A minimal all in one example would be

```julia
julia> struct MyParametricFunc{T} end
julia> "Some docs"
julia> (f::MyParametricFunc{T})() where T = f
julia> @doc (::MyParametricFunc{T})() where T
Some docs
```

This only fixes the name of the documented function, not the signature, but at least it allows signatures including type parameters and typevars.","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/45529/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"timeline_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/45529/timeline","performed_via_github_app":null,"state_reason":null},"comment":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/comments/1154773312","html_url":"https://github.com/JuliaLang/julia/pull/45529#issuecomment-1154773312","issue_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/45529","id":1154773312,"node_id":"IC_kwDOABkWpM5E1HFA","user":{"login":"mortenpi","id":147757,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjE0Nzc1Nw==","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/147757?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/mortenpi","html_url":"https://github.com/mortenpi","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mortenpi/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mortenpi/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mortenpi/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mortenpi/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mortenpi/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mortenpi/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mortenpi/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mortenpi/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mortenpi/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"created_at":"2022-06-14T06:34:00Z","updated_at":"2022-06-14T06:34:00Z","author_association":"CONTRIBUTOR","body":"It might be a good idea to bring this up to date with master and let the tests re-run.
